data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Ends Up 0.76% After Early Rally Fades
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks opened higher after the holiday but gave up part of their gains, with the Hang Seng Index closing up 0.76% at 23,055.03 after earlier rising more than 2% to an intraday high of 23,335.92.

According to Jin10, the Hang Seng Tech Index fell 0.4% to 4,454.28 after turning lower in the afternoon.

Market turnover totaled HK$369.63 billion, up from HK$308.05 billion in the previous session.

On the sector front, computing hardware and semiconductor shares sold off broadly in line with overseas markets, with several stocks dropping more than 10%. Auto, rare earth, and precious metals shares led gains, while the innovative drug sector rose again in the afternoon.

Among individual stocks, BYD Co. rose 8.07%, China Gold International gained 11.43%, JL MAG Rare-Earth climbed 12.03%, and CSPC Pharmaceutical Group advanced 8.32%. GigaDevice Semiconductor fell 18.39%, and Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. declined 10.07%.

data.title: Germany Rejects Trump’s Call for NATO Loyalty to Washington
data.description:
German Defense Minister Boris Pistorius rejected comments by U.S. President Donald Trump that NATO’s European allies should show unconditional loyalty to Washington.

According to Jin10, Pistorius said on Thursday that NATO is not about “blind obedience” and that decisions within the alliance are made through free, unanimous consultations among all member states rather than being determined unilaterally by any single country.

Pistorius also said he expected the United States would not level further criticism at Germany at the NATO summit scheduled for next week in Turkey, following a dispute between German Chancellor Merz and Trump over U.S. strategy in the Iran war.

data.title: SoftBank Corp’s SB NEO to Launch NeoCloud Service for US Enterprises
data.description: SoftBank Group’s SoftBank Corp said its subsidiary SB NEO will launch NeoCloud, a service that will provide computing resources needed for large language model training and inference to major U.S. enterprises, including hyperscale cloud providers, according to 36Kr.

data.title: Shenzhen Xingluo Shiguang Raises Registered Capital by About 51% to RMB 28.38 Billion
data.description: Shenzhen Xingluo Shiguang Technology Co., Ltd. increased its registered capital to about RMB 28.38 billion from about RMB 18.78 billion, a rise of roughly 51%, according to 36Kr, citing Tianyancha. The company was founded in September 2022 and is legally represented by Shi Yulei. Its business scope includes information system integration services, software development, and goods import and export. Tianyancha shows the firm is wholly owned by Douyin Group (Hong Kong) Co., Ltd.

data.title: Li Auto Plans Product-Decision Reshuffle, Moving Key Product Functions Into R&D
data.description:
Li Auto is preparing a new round of organizational restructuring around its product decision-making process, including splitting off some key functions from its product department and folding them into R&D, according to 36Kr. People familiar with the plan said the changes mainly target core product-definition teams for the electric vehicle platform and autonomous driving.

Under the plan, the product department’s electric vehicle platform definition team will be split and moved into the vehicle R&D division led by Liu Liguo, while the autonomous driving terminal product team will be merged into the foundation model R&D team led by Zhan Kun. One source added that some experience-related product staff may also move into a product line overseen by Liu Jie. The product department is led by Fan Haoyu and covers areas including EV platform, spatial intelligence, autonomous driving terminal products, interaction design, platform operations, and the app and website.

data.title: China’s MOFCOM: China-EU Trade and Investment Mechanism to Hold 1-2 Ministerial Meetings a Year
data.description: China’s Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) said China and the European Union have set up a new regular trade and investment consultation mechanism and agreed to hold one to two ministerial-level meetings each year, according to 36Kr. At a regular press briefing on July 2, MOFCOM spokesperson He Yadong said China has invited European Commission Commissioner for Trade and Economic Security Maroš Šefčovič to visit China in the fall of 2026 for the mechanism’s second meeting. He said the first meeting focused on promoting “upward” rather than “downward” trade balance through cooperation in emerging areas such as AI and the green transition, deeper exploration of potential cooperation including services trade, and addressing issues via market-access consultations. He added that both sides reached a consensus on a new positioning for China-EU economic ties as stable and balanced key trade partners.

data.title: Kospi Plunges 7.89% to 7,648.09 in Sharpest Single-Day Drop on Chip Sell-Off
data.description: Yonhap News Agency reported that South Korean stocks plunged nearly 8 percent Thursday, dragged down by a sharp sell-off in semiconductor shares as investor sentiment soured amid renewed concerns over excess AI capacity. The benchmark Kospi plunged 655.32 points, or 7.89 percent, to close at 7,648.09 after falling as low as 7,616.33. The Korea Exchange activated a sell-side sidecar around 9:07 a.m., halting program trading for five minutes after the Kospi 200 Futures index dropped 5 percent or more for at least one minute. Samsung Electronics shed 9.06 percent to 286,000 won, while SK Hynix tumbled 14.57 percent to 2.19 million won. SK Square sank 13.2 percent and Samsung Electro-Mechanics retreated 12.65 percent. Investor sentiment weakened after reports that Meta Platforms was considering selling access to its AI computing infrastructure, fueling concerns the company may have built more capacity than needed. Samsung and SK Hynix also faced pressure after reports that Apple is in talks to source chips from two Chinese semiconductor companies, raising competition concerns. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index tumbled 6.3 percent overnight. "Semiconductor stocks came under pressure after reports that Meta could enter the cloud infrastructure business," said Lee Kyoung-min, an analyst at Daishin Securities. "Reports that Apple is exploring chip sourcing from Chinese semiconductor companies also raised concerns that increased supply could ease supply bottlenecks and weaken chip pricing."Foreigners and institutions sold a net 4.37 trillion won and 2.07 trillion won respectively, while individuals bought a net 6.26 trillion won. Trade volume was 499.85 million shares worth 48.86 trillion won ($31.44 billion). Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said inflation expectations and inflation risks have eased in recent weeks, bolstering hopes for a delay in an interest rate hike.

data.title: Binance Alpha 2.0 Completes Story (IP) Rebrand to Data Network (DATAIP) With 1:1 Token Swap
data.description:
Binance Wallet announced on X that Binance Alpha 2.0 has completed the rebranding plan from Story (IP) to Data Network (DATAIP), alongside a token swap carried out at a 1:1 ratio. The update also stated that deposits and withdrawals for DATAIP tokens are now open, marking the operational transition from IP to DATAIP within Binance Alpha 2.0.

Key Details

The announcement said Binance Alpha 2.0 trading opened for Data Network (DATAIP) at 2026-07-02 08:00 (UTC). It also noted that deposits and withdrawals for DATAIP are available. The post referenced “Data Network (DATAIP) token CA:” but did not provide any contract address details in the text provided.

data.title: Turkey’s Central Bank Launches One-Week Gold-for-Lira Swap Auction
data.description:
Turkey’s central bank launched a one-week “gold-for-lira” sell-side swap auction using a traditional method, with a size of 6 metric tons of gold.

According to Jin10, the auction was initiated through the central bank’s conventional operating framework.

data.title: Yen Jumps Suddenly Against Dollar as Traders Watch for Possible Japan Intervention
data.description:
The Japanese yen suddenly jumped against the U.S. dollar on Thursday, prompting traders to stay alert for possible intervention by Japanese authorities to support the currency.

According to Jin10, it was unclear what triggered the market move and whether Japanese authorities entered the market to intervene.

The size of the move appeared smaller than the swings seen after several previous rounds of intervention.

Hirofumi Suzuki, chief FX strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp., said it was not clear whether the move reflected intervention. He added that, as reported, authorities may have shifted their intervention strategy and tactics and may no longer signal intervention in advance, which could make it harder for the yen to weaken further.

data.title: Google Says EU Court Ruling Overlooked Investment Needed to Keep Android Open
data.description:
Google said a ruling by the European Union’s top court failed to recognize the investment required to keep the Android operating system’s market open.

According to Jin10, the company argued that the decision did not adequately acknowledge the resources needed to maintain Android’s openness.

data.title: BAYC Holder Sells Three NFTs for 27.42 ETH to Add to Ether Long Position, Lookonchain Says
data.description:
A Bored Ape Yacht Club holder known as “Machi Big Brother” sold three BAYC NFTs today for 27.42 ETH to raise funds to increase an Ether long position, according to Lookonchain monitoring.

According to Foresight News, the sale resulted in a loss of 25 ETH.

The wallet is currently holding a long position of 2,600 ETH, with a liquidation price of $1,596.69.

data.title: Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters Warns U.S. and Israel Against Miscalculation
data.description:
Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters warned the United States and Israel not to make a miscalculation.

According to Jin10, the warning was reported in media coverage, without providing further details.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Takes First Flight on Qatar-Gifted Air Force One on July 1
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump took his first flight on a new Air Force One aircraft gifted by the Qatari government on July 1, praising the plane and saying the United States could not build one like it.

According to Odaily, Trump boarded the aircraft before departing for North Dakota to attend the opening event for the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library.

Speaking to reporters before boarding, Trump said he was excited about the first flight and described the aircraft as something people had never seen before.

data.title: Alphabet’s Google Loses EU Court Appeal Over €4.1 Billion Android Antitrust Fine
data.description: Alphabet’s Google lost its legal challenge in an EU court over a €4.1 billion Android antitrust fine. According to Odaily, the ruling means Google was unsuccessful in its lawsuit contesting the penalty.

data.title: Commerzbank Says Market Bets on Another RBA Rate Hike This Year Are Outdated
data.description:
A Commerzbank strategist said the risk backdrop has shifted, arguing that market pricing for another Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) rate hike this year no longer reflects the balance of risks.

According to Jin10, Commerzbank strategist Volkmar Baur said markets were pricing only a 50% chance of another RBA hike this year, a view he said he did not agree with.

Baur said energy-related upside risks were fading while downside risks linked to falling property prices were becoming more visible. He said inflation would need to rise sharply to prompt the RBA to tighten policy again, and he expected that scenario would not occur.

He added that recent risk discussions had focused on Iran’s ongoing conflict and the possibility of elevated fossil-fuel prices. However, he said oil prices had continued to fall since the RBA’s last meeting, meaning that risk was not currently present.

On the downside, Baur highlighted the possibility of a significant decline in property prices, which he said could weigh on consumer spending and slow the economy. He said the latest data suggested this dynamic might be starting to take shape.

Baur said the overall risk landscape appeared to be changing and reiterated that only a sharp rise in inflation would lead the RBA to raise rates again this year, which he did not expect to happen.

data.title: Whale Address Withdraws 5,926 ETH From a CEX Over Two Days, Data Show
data.description:
A wallet identified as 0x2684 withdrew 5,926 ETH from a centralized exchange (CEX) on July 2, according to monitoring data. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the activity was flagged by Lookonchain.

Over the past two days, the same address has withdrawn and staked a total of 15,802 ETH.

data.title: Adani, IHC Sign MOU for $11.5 Billion India Aluminum Project
data.description:
Adani Enterprises Ltd. and Abu Dhabi’s International Holding Co. signed a memorandum of understanding for an aluminum project in India’s eastern state of Odisha, with planned investment of $11.5 billion, according to Bloomberg.

The project would extend billionaire Gautam Adani’s push to expand the conglomerate’s metals business.

data.title: Man Pleads Guilty to Bank Fraud and Identity Theft in $476,000 Vehicle Loan Scheme
data.description:
James Raymond Hanes pleaded guilty to bank fraud and aggravated identity theft after obtaining more than $476,000 in vehicle loans.

According to NS3.AI, prosecutors said Hanes used at least 16 counterfeit driver’s licenses to purchase cars across Northern California.

Authorities said he later resold the vehicles after buying them with the fraudulently secured loans.

data.title: Swiss Franc Pulls Back After Softer Swiss Inflation Data
data.description:
The Swiss franc fell from an intraday high against the U.S. dollar after Switzerland’s consumer price index came in below expectations, though the pair remained within its weekly range and stayed near the 0.8140 area, close to a one-year high.

According to Jin10, Switzerland’s June inflation slowed to 0% month on month from 0.2% in May, a larger deceleration than the market expectation of 0.1%. Year-on-year inflation also eased to 0.5% from 0.6% in May.

The data effectively confirmed expectations that the Swiss National Bank will keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged at the current 0% level for the rest of this year and possibly through 2027. As investors increased bets on U.S. Federal Reserve rate hikes, Switzerland’s low-rate stance was described as a headwind for any rebound in the Swiss franc.

Later today, market attention is set to shift to the U.S. nonfarm payrolls report, which is expected to show the U.S. economy added 110,000 jobs in June after strong employment growth over the prior three months. Investors are expected to assess the data through the lens of monetary policy for signals confirming a Fed rate hike in September. The report said the U.S. dollar faced greater upside risks.
